text:
Once upon a time, in sleepy Willow Creek woodland town lived a curious raccoon named Rusty.

Every morning Rusty would trot over to Whispering Willow Creek — a secret tranquil spot surrounded by taller trees — sip some dew drops from fallen leaves, whistle a happy tune, and look deep into nothingness.

One morning something amazed Rusty. He saw a tiny bird flutter downwards from branch to branch like autumn leaves.

"Haha!" thought Rusty. "How nimble!"

That day he tried mimicking that bird song. He flew downwards chanting his melody. Birds scattered aside, curious? Rusty joined them.

They soared over waterfalls singing happily!

That night rustles filled the air. Creatures great and small gathered.

"Oh," Rusty thought slowly, "maybe they think my song is scary?"

Suddenly something grabbed him!

"No!"

It was Farmer Harris! A giant owl!

"Why owl?" said farmer curiously.

"To call birds!"

"Away owl!"

Farmer Harris laughed heartily
